name: Tangine of Moroccan Vegetables with Couscous
recipeIngredient: 1 each Red bell pepper, seeded, cut into 3/4-inch wide strips
1 each Butternut squash, pared, seeded, and cut into 2-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the tangine: In a large saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the onions and cook, stirring often, until lightly browned, about 6 to 8 minutes.
3. Add the bell pepper, garlic, turmeric, ginger, cinnamon, salt, cayenne, and saffron; stir for 1 minute.
4. Stir in the carrots, squash, tomatoes with their juice, and vegetable stock; break up the tomatoes with a spoon. Bring to a simmer over high heat.
5. Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20 to 30 minutes, until the vegetables are just tender.
6. Stir in the zucchini, garbanzo beans, and raisins; cover and continue cooking until the squash is tender, about 5 to 10 minutes.
7. Make the couscous: In a large saucepan, combine the water, olive oil, and salt; bring to a boil over high heat.
8. Stir in the couscous; immediately remove from the heat, cover, and let stand until the couscous has absorbed all the liquid, about 5 minutes.
9. Place the couscous on a warmed serving platter and make a well in the center.
10. With a slotted spoon, spoon the vegetables into the well.
11. Pour the tangine cooking juices over the couscous, sprinkle with the toasted almonds, and serve.
